reviews.review_msg: Dr. Allen is one of the most caring and approachable professors you will ever encounter at SFU. The course is relatively easy if you do the labs yourself and ask for help when needed. It does demand work. There is a skills assessment, which is basically a final on what is learned throughout all labs. If you are doing the labs, you will be good.

It was his first time teaching this class and he was great! He responded to emails quickly and was eager to help. This class requires students to be proactive to ask for help and if enough effort is put in it can be an easy A. Attend class, ask help when needed for labs, and try and understand what the data means.

Lecture may be disorganized but content is great. Ask him detailed questions and suggestions about what to do, how to do it, and the grading rubric. Communicate with him because he wants you to succeed and is VERY understanding. Go to office hours & ask for help. Graded by 3 papers, rubric is given. Ask for what each section of the rubric implies.

Class itself is easy(A- avg). Just 3 papers. However, Dr. Campbell is disorganized and it seems like he puts together lectures 10min before the class starts. A lecture could be anywhere from 3-30 slides. Slides have 1-3 vague short sentence bullet points. His expectations are unclear. The assignment syllabus is bare, not uploaded until week due.
